steps.title: Understand what we're comparing
steps.content: The question asks: for how many points is actual y greater than predicted y? On the graph, the predicted y-values lie on the line of best fit, so we're looking for data points that are above the line.

steps.title: Estimate the line equation using reference points
steps.content:
To compare each point systematically, we need to know where the line passes. Reading from the graph:
  • At $x = 0$, the line is at approximately $y = 84$
  • At $x = 10$, the line is at approximately $y = 97$
Slope: $\frac{97 - 84}{10 - 0} = \frac{13}{10} = 1.3$
Line equation: $y \approx 1.3x + 84$

steps.title: Check each point: is actual y greater than line's y-value?
steps.content:
For each data point at position $x$, compare its actual $y$-coordinate to the line's predicted value $1.3x + 84$:
  • $(4, 90)$: Line predicts $1.3(4) + 84 = 89.2$; actual is $90 > 89.2$ ✓ ABOVE
  • $(5, 88)$: Line predicts $1.3(5) + 84 = 90.5$; actual is $88 ot> 90.5$ (below)
  • $(6, 92)$: Line predicts $1.3(6) + 84 = 91.8$; actual is $92 > 91.8$ ✓ ABOVE
  • $(6, 90)$: Line predicts $91.8$; actual is $90 ot> 91.8$ (below)
  • $(7, 93)$: Line predicts $1.3(7) + 84 = 93.1$; actual is $93 ot> 93.1$ (below)
  • $(7, 91)$: Line predicts $93.1$; actual is $91 ot> 93.1$ (below)
  • $(8, 95)$: Line predicts $1.3(8) + 84 = 94.4$; actual is $95 > 94.4$ ✓ ABOVE
  • $(9, 97)$: Line predicts $1.3(9) + 84 = 95.7$; actual is $97 > 95.7$ ✓ ABOVE
  • $(10, 100)$: Line predicts $1.3(10) + 84 = 97$; actual is $100 > 97$ ✓ ABOVE

steps.title: Count points above the line
steps.content: The points above the line are at $x = 4, 6, 8, 9,$ and $10$ — that is 5 points where actual $y$ exceeds predicted $y$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
